name: Beaches and fields of Cambrils
description:
In the heart of the Costa Daurada, the coastal town of Cambrils maintains a double personality. On the one hand, the fishing and maritime tradition of its port makes it a top-level tourist destination, with beaches and restaurants where you can enjoy the sea and Mediterranean cuisine. But it is enough to go a little inland to discover a rural character that survives thanks to its excellent soil, and which still preserves an important agricultural tradition. There you find an agrarian mosaic in which woody crops such as olive, hazelnut, almond and carob trees predominate, but also an irrigated fruit and vegetable garden. It is an easy Route that can be done with mountain or gravel bikes, to be able to circulate without problem on the dirt tracks of the interior.

The Route begins just in front of the port of Cambrils, from where you continue south along its promenade. You pass by different beaches of the municipality, such as the one of the Llosa or the one of l'Ardiaca. When you reach the end of the Riudecanyes stream, turn inland. After passing under the train tracks and the highway, the landscape changes radically. You leave behind the most touristic urbanization to enter the so-called Plana del Baix Camp, which is characterized by its eminently agricultural character. Between fields you arrive at the height of Mont-roig del Camp, where you turn towards the east, to start the return towards Cambrils.

Shortly after, the Route passes the Teixells castle, the old San Rafael farmhouse, built in exposed stone and excellently preserved. Teixells was previously a segregated municipality, center of the fertile plain of the Plana del Baix Camp. Due to its importance and being at a strategic crossroads, the farmhouse was fortified to defend itself against attacks. When you get to Cambrils, you pass by the Cambrils History Museum. It is impossible to get confused, since it is presided over by the Torre del Port, an old watchtower that since the 17th century was in charge of protecting the port from pirate attacks. You can finish the day with a bath and eating in one of the many seafood restaurants that are next to the port.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cambrils offers diverse landscapes, combining a unique coastline with pre-coastal mountain ranges. The terrain features Mediterranean forests, extensive agricultural areas with vineyards and olive groves, and a network of rural paths. Routes transition from flat coastal sections to challenging ascents in areas like the Sierra de Llaberia and La Mussara, providing varied riding experiences. This region provides a mix of terrains suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cambrils The most popular mountain bike trail is Loop around Baix Camp from Cambrils, a 26.6 miles (42.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the varied rural landscapes of the Baix Camp region. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Beaches and fields of Cambrils, an easy 14.1 miles (22.6 km) path. This route offers a relaxed ride through coastal areas and agricultural fields. Local mountain bikers also love the Centenary Pine of l'Aleixar – Mas del Victor loop from Cambrils, a 36.8 miles (59.3 km) trail leading through Mediterranean forests and rural areas, often completed in about 5 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Cambrils is defined by its coastal paths, agricultural fields, and pre-coastal mountain ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to difficult mountain challenges. The routes in Cambrils are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 2300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cambrils's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cambrils?
A: Cambrils offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with over 120 routes available. These range from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cambrils?
A: The terrain around Cambrils is incredibly diverse, offering a unique blend of coastal paths, extensive agricultural areas with vineyards and olive groves, and challenging pre-coastal mountain ranges. You'll find everything from flat, easy rides along the sea to demanding ascents in areas like the Sierra de Llaberia and La Mussara.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike routes in Cambrils?
A: Yes, Cambrils has several easy and family-friendly options. For a relaxed ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220159061/beaches-and-fields-of-cambrils" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beaches and fields of Cambrils&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.6 km long and offers a gentle ride through coastal areas and agricultural fields. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33755659" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrils Seafront Promenade loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt;, which is 22.0 km and stays close to the coast.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14912145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centenary Pine of l'Aleixar – Mas del Victor loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 59.3 km trail that takes about 5 hours 30 minutes to complete, leading through Mediterranean forests and rural areas with considerable climbing.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that offer scenic views or points of interest?
A: Absolutely. Many routes combine sport with culture and panoramic views. The region boasts stunning natural features and historical sites. For example, you can explore routes that lead to places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery&lt;/a&gt; or offer incredible panoramic views from the Ermita de la Mare de Déu de la Roca. The contrast of sea and mountain landscapes provides constant scenic beauty.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cambrils?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cambrils are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the unique combination of coastal and mountain landscapes, and the well-maintained rural paths that allow for diverse riding experiences.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that are circular?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Cambrils are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955113006/loop-around-baix-camp-from-cambrils" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop around Baix Camp from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33755659" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrils Seafront Promenade loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region around Cambrils is rich in history and offers routes that pass by impressive historical sites. You can find trails leading to castles such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/226370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Castle, Colldejou&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escornalbou Castle-Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, providing a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cambrils?
A: Cambrils is an excellent destination for outdoor activities year-round. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying mild weather. Winter can also be a good option, with cooler temperatures suitable for longer rides.

Q: Are there mountain passes or significant climbs for road biking in the area?
A: While this guide focuses on mountain biking, the region is also known for its challenging mountain passes that are popular with road cyclists. Notable climbs include the Coll de la Teixeta, offering spectacular views of the Costa Daurada, and routes towards La Mussara, which provide significant elevation gains from sea level.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Cambrils?
A: You'll encounter a diverse range of natural features, from the Mediterranean coastline and its sea breeze to lush Mediterranean forests and extensive agricultural lands filled with vineyards and olive groves. The interior also features mountainous formations and rural paths that often run alongside streams or through fields.

Q: Are there any routes that explore wine regions near Cambrils?
A: Yes, some routes allow you to combine mountain biking with enotourism. For instance, routes connecting Cambrils to the Priorat region offer the chance to explore one of Catalonia's most beautiful wine-producing areas, passing through historic villages and vineyards.
tips: The cozy and authentic fishing village is known for the relaxed atmosphere that the life of fishermen brings with it. That quiet atmosphere is interrupted every working day around 17:00 by the fish auction in the harbor. Known in the far area for the high-quality fish that is sold here.
Beautiful coastal path. With a separate cycle path. There are plenty of places to rest and regular cafes.
The farmhouse of Sant Rafael, also known as Castell dels Teixells, is a very well-preserved building located on the Camino de los Tegells, in Cambrils.

A really pretty port in the middle of Cambrils promenade. Simply gorgeous by day and by night. You have the opportunity to walk left and right along a path along the water and get a great view over the Mediterranean Sea. Also with a great history for the city of Cambrils. Always worth a walk! Here you can also use a ferry to Salou and rent water sports activities such as jet skiing and boats.
